title: PaperParser graph.json schema
description: Schema for the graph file of a PaperParser bundle. Encodes mathematical objects (nodes) and logical dependencies (edges) as a directed graph.

schema_version.description: Semver version of the PaperParser schema specification.

nodes.description: Mathematical objects extracted from the paper.

node_id.description: Canonical node ID in the format sec{N}::{kind_abbrev}:{label}. The label component must use only lowercase ASCII letters, digits, and hyphens.

edge_kind.description: The type of logical dependency between nodes.

evidence_level.description: How the dependency was determined: 'explicit_ref' (stated in text), 'inferred' (logically necessary but not cited), 'external' (references an outside source).

proof_status.description: Status of the proof: 'full' (complete proof given), 'sketch' (proof sketch only), 'deferred' (proof in appendix or later), 'external' (proved elsewhere), 'not_applicable' (definitions, assumptions, etc.).

novelty.description: Novelty classification: 'new' (first in this paper), 'classical' (well-known), 'extended' (known with new proof/extension), 'folklore' (widely believed but not formally published).

node.description: A mathematical object extracted from the paper.

label.description: Human-readable label as it appears in the paper (e.g., 'Theorem 4.1 (Main convergence result)'). Include the parenthetical name if the paper provides one.

section.description: Section number (e.g., '2', '3.1', 'A'). Use '0' for preamble/front matter.

section_title.description: Title of the section. Empty string if the section is untitled.

number.description: The theorem/definition number as printed in the paper (e.g., '2.1', 'A.3'). Empty string if unnumbered.

latex_label.description: The LaTeX \label{} tag if available from source. null if unavailable (e.g., PDF source).

statement.description: The full mathematical statement in LaTeX-flavored plain text. Include all quantifiers, hypotheses, and conclusions.

is_main_result.description: true if this is one of the paper's headline results (typically mentioned in the abstract or introduction).

metadata.description: Free-form object for additional information (e.g., proof_length_lines, original_label, source, page, tags). May be empty {}.

edge.description: A logical dependency between two mathematical objects.

source.description: Node ID of the source (the 'from' node). Must reference an existing node.

target.description: Node ID of the target (the 'to' node). Must reference an existing node.

detail.description: A short natural-language explanation of the dependency. Be specific: mention proof steps, equation numbers, or the technique used.

metadata.description: Free-form object for additional information (e.g., proof_step, equation_ref). May be empty {}.
